programs.name: B.Tech Computer Science & Engineering

hero.description: Build a strong foundation in computing, AI, and modern software technologies.

overview: The B.Tech in Electronics & Communication Engineering explores the design, development, and deployment of electronic devices, circuits, and communication systems that underpin modern life. From mobile networks and satellite communications to embedded controllers and IoT devices, the programme builds expertise across analogue and digital electronics, signal processing, wireless systems, VLSI technology, and microcontroller-based design. Students gain fluency in both hardware and software aspects of electronic engineering through an integrated curriculum of theory, laboratory practice, and project work.

Bengaluru’s position as a centre for semiconductor manufacturing, telecom R&D, and electronics innovation gives students proximity to major employers and research organisations. The programme incorporates industry-oriented certifications, collaborative projects, and internship placements that connect classroom learning to professional practice. Graduates emerge prepared for roles in telecommunications, semiconductor design, consumer electronics, aerospace, defence, and automation industries worldwide.

faq.q: What is eligibility?
faq.a: 10+2 with PCM.

faq.q: How do international students apply for the B.Tech CSE programme?
faq.a: International applicants should visit the Presidency University International admissions portal or contact the Office of International Affairs directly. The process typically involves submitting academic transcripts, proof of English language proficiency, and relevant entrance examination scores. Admission considers JEE Main, KCET, COMEDK, or the university’s own evaluation test.

faq.q: What entrance examinations are accepted?
faq.a: The university accepts JEE Main, JEE Advanced, Karnataka CET (KCET), COMEDK UGET, and the Presidency University Entrance Examination Test (PUEET). International applicants should check with the admissions team whether alternative qualifications are accepted.

faq.q: What career prospects are available after graduation?
faq.a: Graduates find roles as software developers, data scientists, AI engineers, cybersecurity specialists, cloud architects, and more across multinational IT companies, product firms, start-ups, government research bodies, and consulting organisations. The university’s placement cell maintains strong industry connections with regular on-campus recruitment drives.

faq.q: Is the B.Tech CSE programme in demand globally?
faq.a: Yes, B.Tech in Computer Science & Engineering remains one of the most in-demand degrees worldwide due to the growing need for tech professionals across every industry. Graduates are hired for roles spanning software development, data science, AI, and cybersecurity, with strong salary prospects and global career mobility.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University offers dedicated student housing on its Bengaluru campus. International students are encouraged to contact the Office of International Affairs for information on hostel availability, room types, and the booking process.

programs.name: B.Tech Computer Science & Technology (Big Data)

hero.description: Harness the power of large-scale data to drive decisions that shape industries. This four-year specialisation equips you with the tools and techniques to collect, process, analyse, and visualise complex datasets — preparing you for one of the fastest-growing career fields in global technology.

overview: Data is expanding at an unprecedented pace, and the ability to extract actionable insights from complex datasets is now a core requirement across virtually every industry. The B.Tech in Computer Science & Technology with a specialisation in Big Data is an interdisciplinary programme that fuses principles from statistics, computer science, and domain-specific applications to prepare graduates for roles as qualified data engineers and analysts.

The programme combines foundational computer science with deep expertise in mathematics, statistical analysis, computer programming, and software design — all oriented towards the effective management and interpretation of digital data at scale. Students work with industry-standard tools and platforms including Python, R, Hadoop, Apache Spark, TensorFlow, and advanced analytics frameworks, building competencies that align directly with global employer demand.

faq.q: How does the Big Data specialisation differ from the core CSE programme?
faq.a: While the core CSE programme offers broad coverage across all computing disciplines, the Big Data specialisation provides deep expertise in large-scale data processing, analytics, machine learning, and data visualisation. Students gain hands-on experience with platforms like Hadoop, Spark, and TensorFlow alongside core computing fundamentals.

faq.q: What tools and technologies will I learn?
faq.a: : The programme covers Python, R, SQL, Hadoop, Apache Spark, TensorFlow, cloud analytics platforms (AWS, Google Cloud, Azure), Power BI, Tableau, and various machine learning and deep learning frameworks.

faq.q: What are the eligibility requirements for international applicants?
faq.a: Applicants must have completed 10+2 or equivalent with Physics and Mathematics as compulsory subjects, along with Chemistry or Computer Science or related subjects, with at least 45% marks combined. Entrance through JEE, KCET, COMEDK, or PUEET.

faq.q: Are there industry certification opportunities during the programme?
faq.a: Yes. Students can earn globally recognised certifications including AWS Data Analytics, Google Data Engineer, and TensorFlow Developer credentials alongside their degree.

faq.q: What is the typical career trajectory for graduates?
faq.a: Graduates typically begin as data engineers or analysts, progressing to senior data scientist, lead data architect, or analytics manager roles. The specialisation also provides a strong foundation for postgraduate research in data science and AI.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University offers dedicated student housing on its Bengaluru campus. International students are encouraged to contact the Office of International Affairs for information on hostel availability, room types, and the booking process.

programs.name: B.Tech Computer Science & Technology (DevOps)

hero.description: Master the principles and tools that power modern software delivery. This four-year specialisation in DevOps engineering prepares you to design CI/CD pipelines, automate infrastructure, and manage cloud-native environments — skills that are critical to the way global technology organisations build and deploy software today.

overview: DevOps has become a foundational practice in how organisations build, test, deploy, and maintain software systems at scale. This specialisation integrates the principles of software development and IT operations, equipping graduates with the skills to create automated, reliable, and scalable delivery pipelines that accelerate the entire software development lifecycle

The curriculum covers CI/CD pipeline design, containerisation with Docker and Kubernetes, infrastructure automation using Terraform and Ansible, cloud computing on AWS and other platforms, monitoring and observability tools, and version control systems. Students develop proficiency in software development and project management methodologies, learning to streamline workflows and optimise collaboration between development and operations teams.

faq.q: How does DevOps differ from traditional software engineering?
faq.a: DevOps integrates software development with IT operations to accelerate delivery, improve reliability, and enable continuous improvement. While traditional software engineering focuses on coding and design, DevOps encompasses the entire pipeline from development through deployment and monitoring.

faq.q: What tools will I learn in this programme?
faq.a: : The curriculum covers Jenkins, Docker, Kubernetes, Ansible, Terraform, AWS, Git/GitHub, monitoring tools (Prometheus, Grafana), and CI/CD platforms — the same tools used by leading technology organisations globally.

faq.q: What are the eligibility requirements for international applicants?
faq.a: Applicants must have completed 10+2 or equivalent with Physics and Mathematics as compulsory subjects, along with Chemistry or Computer Science or related subjects, with at least 45% marks combined.

faq.q: : What is the demand for DevOps professionals?
faq.a: DevOps is one of the fastest-growing engineering disciplines globally. As organisations accelerate cloud migration and software delivery, demand for skilled DevOps engineers continues to outpace supply across all major technology markets.

faq.q: Can I pursue further studies after this programme?
faq.a: Yes. Graduates are well-positioned for M.Tech or MS programmes in cloud computing, software engineering, or distributed systems at universities worldwide. The programme also provides a foundation for industry certifications that enhance career progression.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University offers dedicated student housing on its Bengaluru campus. International students are encouraged to contact the Office of International Affairs for information on hostel availability, room types, and the booking process.

programs.name: B.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (AI & ML)

hero.description: Step into the field that is reshaping every industry on theplanet. This four-year specialisation combines mathematics, programming, statistics, and software design to develop your ability to build intelligent systems that learn, reason, and adapt preparing you for high-impact careers in AI research, data science, and automation

overview: Artificial Intelligence is a branch of computer science focused on enabling machines to perform tasks that typically require human intelligence including rational thinking, perception, and decision-making. Machine Learning extends this by enabling systems to teach themselves from data, improving their performance without explicit programming. This programme fuses these disciplines into a comprehensive engineering education.

The curriculum combines core domains of mathematics, computer programming, machine learning algorithms, statistical modelling, and software design to develop the capacity to replicate and extend human cognitive processes omputationally. Students work with industry-standard platforms including TensorFlow, PyTorch, and cloud-based AI services, building competencies in deep learning, neural networks, NLP, computer vision, and reinforcement learning. Graduates are recruited by multinational IT companies, large Indian technology organisations, AI-focused product firms, and research institutions.

programs.name: B.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (Blockchain)

hero.description: Enter the world of decentralised technology that is transforming finance, supply chains, governance, and digital identity. This four-year specialisation combines computer science fundamentals with deep expertise in cryptography, distributed ledger technology, and smart contract development — preparing you for careers at the intersection of security, innovation, and digital transformation.

overview: Blockchain technology is a decentralised, distributed ledger system that securely records the provenance and transfer of digital assets. It has moved well beyond cryptocurrency to become a foundational technology across financial services, supply chain management, healthcare records, government services, and digital identity verification.

This specialisation combines core domains of mathematics,computer programming, software design, and information security to prepare graduates for roles as blockchain developers and solution architects. The curriculum covers smart contracts, decentralised applications (DApps), cryptographic protocols, consensus mechanisms, peer-to-peer networking, and leading blockchain platforms including Ethereum, Hyperledger, and Corda. Graduates are positioned for careers in Web3, FinTech, cyber security, and enterprise blockchain solutions with multinational IT companies and emerging technology firms.

faq.q: What makes blockchain a promising career field?
faq.a: Blockchain technology is being adopted across finance, supply chain, healthcare, government, and digital identity sectors worldwide. Demand for blockchain developers and architects continues to grow as organisations implement decentralised solutions for security, transparency, and efficiency.

faq.q: How does this specialisation differ from B.Tech CSE (Cyber Security)?
faq.a: While Cyber Security focuses on protecting systems and data from threats, Blockchain specialises in building decentralised, distributed systems using cryptographic protocols. Both share elements of cryptography and security, but Blockchain is oriented towards building new systems rather than defending existing ones

faq.q: What are the eligibility requirements for international applicants?
faq.a: Applicants must have completed 10+2 or equivalent with Physics and Mathematics as compulsory subjects, along with Chemistry or Computer Science or related subjects, with at least 45% marks combined.

faq.q: What programming languages will I learn?
faq.a: The programme covers Solidity (for smart contracts), JavaScript, Python, Go, and other languages commonly used in blockchain development, alongside core programming fundamentals.

hero.description: : Accelerate your data career with focused, practical training. Master the theory and application of data analytics, processing, and visualisation using the tools and platforms that power data-driven organisations in finance, healthcare, retail, and technology.

overview: This diploma programme combines essential theory with intensive practical skills development in big data technologies. The curriculum covers machine learning, cloud computing, distributed processing with Hadoop and Spark, and industry-expert-designed modules that prepare graduates for immediate contribution to data-driven organisations worldwide.

Students work across the full data lifecycle from data ingestion and storage through processing, analysis, and visualisation. The programme delivers proficiency in Hadoop, Spark, Python, R, SQL, Power BI, Tableau, and cloud data platforms on AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud Platform, giving graduates a comprehensive and immediately deployable skill set.

faq.q: What is the difference between the PG Diploma in Big Data and the M.Tech in Data Science?
faq.a: The PG Diploma is a shorter, practice-focused programme emphasising hands-on big data tools and deployment skills. The M.Tech is a longer, research-intensive degree with deeper theoretical foundations and a dissertation component. The diploma is ideal for professionals seeking rapid skill development; the M.Tech suits those pursuing research or advanced academic credentials.

faq.q: What are the eligibility requirements?
faq.a: : Applicants typically need a recognised undergraduate degree in engineering, computer science, or a related discipline. Specific eligibility criteria for international applicants should be confirmed with the admissions team.

faq.q: AIs prior programming experience required?
faq.a: Familiarity with at least one programming language is recommended. The programme covers Python, R, and SQL from foundational levels, but prior exposure to programming concepts will help students progress more quickly.

faq.q: Which organisations hire PG Diploma graduates?
faq.a: IT services companies, data analytics firms, e-commerce organisations, financial institutions, healthcare providers, and technology startups actively seek professionals with big data skills.

faq.q: Can I pursue further studies after completing the diploma?
faq.a: Yes. The diploma can serve as a stepping stone to M.Tech or MS programmes in data science, big data, or related fields. Some graduates also pursue professional certifications to complement their diploma qualification.

faq.q: : Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University provides student housing. Contact the Office of International Affairs for details..

programs.name: B.Tech Electronics & Communication Engineering

hero.description: Build a strong foundation in computing, AI, and modern software technologies.

overview: The B.Tech in Electronics & Communication Engineering explores the design, development, and deployment of electronic devices, circuits, and communication systems that underpin modern life. From mobile networks and satellite communications to embedded controllers and IoT devices, the programme builds expertise across analogue and digital electronics, signal processing, wireless systems, VLSI technology, and microcontroller-based design. Students gain fluency in both hardware and software aspects of electronic engineering through an integrated curriculum of theory, laboratory practice, and project work.

Bengaluru’s position as a centre for semiconductor manufacturing, telecom R&D, and electronics innovation gives students proximity to major employers and research organisations. The programme incorporates industry-oriented certifications, collaborative projects, and internship placements that connect classroom learning to professional practice. Graduates emerge prepared for roles in telecommunications, semiconductor design, consumer electronics, aerospace, defence, and automation industries worldwide.

faq.q: What is eligibility?
faq.a: 10+2 with PCM.

faq.q: How do international students apply for the B.Tech CSE programme?
faq.a: International applicants should visit the Presidency University International admissions portal or contact the Office of International Affairs directly. The process typically involves submitting academic transcripts, proof of English language proficiency, and relevant entrance examination scores. Admission considers JEE Main, KCET, COMEDK, or the university’s own evaluation test.

faq.q: What entrance examinations are accepted?
faq.a: The university accepts JEE Main, JEE Advanced, Karnataka CET (KCET), COMEDK UGET, and the Presidency University Entrance Examination Test (PUEET). International applicants should check with the admissions team whether alternative qualifications are accepted.

faq.q: What career prospects are available after graduation?
faq.a: Graduates find roles as software developers, data scientists, AI engineers, cybersecurity specialists, cloud architects, and more across multinational IT companies, product firms, start-ups, government research bodies, and consulting organisations. The university’s placement cell maintains strong industry connections with regular on-campus recruitment drives.

faq.q: Is the B.Tech CSE programme in demand globally?
faq.a: Yes, B.Tech in Computer Science & Engineering remains one of the most in-demand degrees worldwide due to the growing need for tech professionals across every industry. Graduates are hired for roles spanning software development, data science, AI, and cybersecurity, with strong salary prospects and global career mobility.

faq.q: Is on-campus accommodation available for international students?
faq.a: Presidency University offers dedicated student housing on its Bengaluru campus. International students are encouraged to contact the Office of International Affairs for information on hostel availability, room types, and the booking process.

programs.name: B.Tech Electrical & Electronics Engineering

hero.description: Power the world’s critical infrastructure. This four-year programme covers electrical machines, power systems, control engineering, and electronics — preparing you for careers in energy generation, distribution, automation, and the growing electric vehicle sector.

overview: The B.Tech in Electrical & Electronics Engineering offers a distinctive blend of electrical, electronics, and computer-related coursework that prepares graduates for careers across power systems, automation, renewable energy, and electronics. The curriculum covers foundation courses in electrical machines, control systems, power electronics, microprocessors, programming, and energy systems — equipping students with the versatility to work across both traditional electrical engineering and emerging technology domains.

India’s rapidly expanding power sector, its ambitious renewable energy targets, and the accelerating adoption of electric vehicles make this a programme with strong long-term career prospects. Students benefit from laboratory facilities that simulate real-world electrical systems, alongside internship opportunities with power utilities, automation firms, and electronics manufacturers. The programme builds graduates who can design, implement, and manage the electrical and electronic systems that underpin modern infrastructure, industry, and transportation.

faq.q: What is the difference between EEE and ECE at Presidency?
faq.a: EEE focuses on electrical power generation, distribution, control systems, and energy management. ECE concentrates on electronic devices, communication systems, signal processing, and VLSI design. EEE graduates typically enter power, energy, and automation sectors, while ECE graduates tend toward telecom, semiconductor, and electronics industries.

faq.q: Is EEE relevant for the electric vehicle industry?
faq.a: Highly relevant. EEE graduates bring expertise in power electronics, motor control, battery management systems, and electrical drivetrain design — all critical competencies for the rapidly growing electric vehicle sector in India and globally.

faq.q: Can EEE graduates work in the renewable energy sector?
faq.a: Yes. The programme covers power systems, energy conversion, and grid management — all foundational to careers in solar energy, wind power, energy storage, and sustainable infrastructure development.

faq.q: Are government sector jobs accessible to EEE graduates?
faq.a: Yes. EEE graduates are eligible for Indian Engineering Services (IES) examinations and recruitment by public sector undertakings including BHEL, NTPC, ONGC, Power Grid Corporation, and ISRO. International students should check specific eligibility requirements for government

faq.q: What programming languages are covered in the EEE curriculum?
faq.a: The programme includes C, C++, Python, and MATLAB for tasks including automation, system modelling, simulation, data analysis, and embedded systems programming.

programs.name: B.Tech VLSI

hero.description: Design the chips powering tomorrow’s technology. This specialised four-year programme focuses on integrated circuit design, semiconductor physics, FPGA prototyping, and electronic system verification — preparing you for careers at the heart of the global semiconductor industry.

overview: The B.Tech in VLSI (Very Large Scale Integration) provides focused training in the design, verification, and fabrication of integrated circuits and electronic systems. The curriculum covers semiconductor device physics, microelectronics, IC fabrication technology, CMOS circuit design, ASIC design, design verification, and FPGA-based embedded systems — all taught using industry-leading EDA (Electronic Design Automation) tools. This programme directly addresses the growing global demand for chip designers and verification engineers as the semiconductor industry expands across consumer electronics, automotive, healthcare, telecommunications, and computing.

Students progress from foundational electronics and programming in early semesters to advanced topics including digital and analogue VLSI design, HDL-based system design, microcontroller architectures, and signal processing. Laboratory work with tools from Cadence, Synopsys, Xilinx, and Mentor Graphics provides hands-on experience with the same platforms used by professional chip design teams. Bengaluru’s established semiconductor ecosystem — home to design centres for major global chipmakers — gives students proximity to internship and employment opportunities in the industry.

faq.q: What is the difference between B.Tech VLSI and B.Tech ECE at Presidency?
faq.a: B.Tech VLSI is a specialised programme focusing specifically on integrated circuit design, semiconductor technology, and chip verification. ECE provides broader coverage of electronics and communication systems including VLSI as one component alongside wireless communication, signal processing, and IoT. VLSI graduates typically enter semiconductor-focused roles.

faq.q: Which EDA tools are used in the VLSI programme?
faq.a: Students work with industry-standard tools including Cadence Virtuoso, Synopsys Design Compiler, Xilinx Vivado, and Mentor Graphics platforms for circuit design, simulation, synthesis, and verification.

faq.q: Is the VLSI programme relevant given the growth of semiconductor manufacturing in India?
faq.a: Highly relevant. India’s semiconductor industry is expanding significantly with new fabrication and design facilities being established. The demand for trained VLSI designers and verification engineers is projected to grow substantially, making this one of the most career-strategic engineering specialisations available.

faq.q: What salary range can VLSI graduates expect?
faq.a: Starting compensation for VLSI roles in India typically ranges from INR 4–8 LPA, with experienced professionals earning significantly more. International positions in semiconductor firms may offer different compensation structures depending on role and location.

faq.q: Can VLSI graduates pursue higher studies?
faq.a: Yes. B.Tech VLSI graduates are well-positioned for M.Tech and MS programmes in VLSI design, embedded systems, semiconductor technology, and related fields at institutions in India and internationally. The programme also provides a foundation for doctoral research in semiconductor technology.

programs.name: B.Tech Civil Engineering

hero.description: Shape the built environment. This four-year programme covers structural design, geotechnical engineering, transportation systems, and sustainable construction — preparing you to plan and build the infrastructure that connects communities and drives economic development.

overview: The B.Tech in Civil Engineering provides comprehensive training in the planning, design, construction, and maintenance of physical infrastructure. The programme covers structural engineering, geotechnical engineering, transportation engineering, water resources, environmental engineering, and construction management. Students develop proficiency in mathematics, applied sciences, and engineering technology to analyse and design structures, systems, and processes that serve society. The curriculum combines classroom instruction with laboratory practice, site visits, and design projects that simulate professional civil engineering challenges.

India’s massive infrastructure investment programme — spanning highways, railways, smart cities, metro systems, airports, and urban development — creates sustained demand for qualified civil engineers. International students gain exposure to large-scale construction practices, emerging sustainable building technologies, and project management methodologies. The programme prepares graduates for careers with construction firms, consulting practices, government agencies, infrastructure developers, and international engineering organisations.

programs.name: B.Tech Mechanical Engineering

hero.description: Engineer the machines and systems that drive industry. This four-year programme covers thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, machine design, manufacturing, and automation — preparing you for careers across automotive, aerospace, energy, robotics, and advanced manufacturing sectors.

overview: The B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering delivers a comprehensive foundation across the core domains of mechanical science — thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, machine design, manufacturing processes, materials science, and mechatronics. The curriculum blends fundamental theory with hands-on experience in product design, prototyping, computer-aided engineering, and industrial automation. Students develop the analytical, computational, and design skills needed to conceive, develop, and manufacture mechanical systems ranging from precision components to large-scale industrial machinery.

Bengaluru’s diverse industrial landscape — spanning automotive manufacturing, aerospace, heavy engineering, robotics, and clean energy — provides students with direct access to internship and employment opportunities across multiple sectors. The programme includes laboratory work with CNC machines, 3D printers, CAD/CAM software, and simulation tools, along with industrial visits that connect classroom concepts to professional engineering practice. Graduates emerge as versatile engineers capable of contributing to manufacturing, product development, energy systems, and emerging technology domains worldwide.

programs.name: B.Tech Petroleum Engineering

hero.description: Engineer the machines and systems that drive industry. This four-year programme covers thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, machine design, manufacturing, and automation — preparing you for careers across automotive, aerospace, energy, robotics, and advanced manufacturing sectors.

overview: The B.Tech in Petroleum Engineering is a specialised multidisciplinary programme encompassing both upstream and downstream sectors of the oil and gas industry. The curriculum covers petroleum exploration, drilling engineering, reservoir management, production operations, digital oil fields, pipeline systems, refining technology, and health and safety — providing comprehensive training across the full petroleum value chain. Students develop expertise in subsurface evaluation, well system design, drilling operations, fluid handling, and resource valuation using geoscientific and engineering methods.

Presidency University is one of very few institutions in Bengaluru offering a dedicated petroleum engineering programme, giving students access to a distinctive academic experience alongside the city’s technology and industrial ecosystem. The department maintains specialised laboratory facilities including drilling simulation equipment, fluid mechanics testing apparatus, and reservoir modelling tools. Industry connections with major oil and gas companies including Shell and Schlumberger facilitate internship placements and graduate recruitment. The programme also incorporates project economics and resource valuation, equipping graduates with the technical and commercial skills demanded by the global energy sector.

programs.name: M.Tech Embedded System & VLSI

hero.description: Advance your expertise in semiconductor design and embedded computing. This two-year postgraduate programme provides specialised training in VLSI design methodology, FPGA development, SoC architecture, and embedded systems — preparing you for senior roles in the global semiconductor industry.

overview: This two-year postgraduate programme focuses on the advances and applications of VLSI design methodology from a systems perspective. The curriculum covers EDA tool development, library design, analogue and mixed-signal design, semiconductor chip design, IC design and verification, FPGA development, and System-onChip (SoC) architecture. Students learn to analyse, design, and implement integrated circuits using industry-standard tools, developing the practical skills that make them immediately productive in professional chip design environments. The programme also enables students to formulate research problems and develop solutions in VLSI design and embedded systems.

The programme integrates core VLSI design principles with emerging trends in IoT, AI, and power-efficient electronics to meet current industry demands. Students access advanced laboratories equipped with tools for VLSI design, FPGA prototyping, circuit simulation, and embedded system testing. Faculty members bring deep experience in semiconductor design and embedded systems, providing both academic rigour and practical industry insight. Bengaluru’s concentration of semiconductor design centres — for companies ranging from Intel and Qualcomm to Indian design houses — creates a rich environment for internship placements and post-graduation employment.

overview: This two-year postgraduate programme provides advanced knowledge in building and construction technology, covering structural systems, construction materials, project management, sustainability, and digital construction methodologies. The curriculum bridges the gap between traditional civil engineering education and the evolving demands of the modern construction industry, where Building Information Modelling (BIM), green building standards, and advanced materials are transforming how infrastructure is designed, built, and maintained.

India’s construction sector is one of the largest globally, driven by massive government investment in smart cities, housing, transportation infrastructure, and industrial development. International students gain access to large-scale construction projects and emerging practices in sustainable building, alongside academic instruction grounded in both theory and professional application. The programme prepares graduates for senior roles in construction management, structural consulting, real estate development, and infrastructure project delivery.

title.normal: Post Graduate Diploma in Drone Technology

hero.description: Launch your career in unmanned aerial systems. This one-year programme — developed in partnership with Garuda Aerospace — covers drone design, operation, AI-driven data analysis, and regulatory compliance, preparing you for one of the fastest-growing technology sectors in aviation, agriculture, infrastructure, and environmental monitoring.

overview: This innovative one-year postgraduate diploma opens doors to the rapidly growing unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) industry. Developed in collaboration with Garuda Aerospace — a leading Indian drone company — the programme provides industry-grade training covering drone system design, aerodynamics, flight control, sensor integration, aerial surveying, AI-driven data processing, and regulatory compliance. Students train at a dedicated Centre of Excellence in Drone Technologies established through the university’s partnership with Garuda Aerospace, gaining access to real-world R&D opportunities and advanced prototyping facilities.

The programme addresses a growing skills gap as drone technology transforms industries including agriculture, logistics, infrastructure inspection, environmental monitoring, defence, and entertainment. Students progress from fundamental principles of UAV design through to advanced applications including photogrammetry, GIS-based analysis, 3D modelling, and autonomous flight systems. The curriculum incorporates hands-on field training in aerial surveying and asset inspection, alongside theoretical coursework in drone regulations, safety protocols, and commercial deployment strategies. Whether you are a professional looking to transition into UAV technology or a graduate seeking a career in emerging aerospace applications, this diploma provides focused, industry-ready skills.

faq.a: Candidates need a recognised undergraduate degree with a minimum of 50% marks (45% for SC/ST candidates from Karnataka). The programme accepts graduates from engineering, science, and related disciplines.

faq.q: Is prior drone flying experience required?
faq.a: No. The programme covers drone operation from foundational principles through to advanced flight training. Prior experience is beneficial but not required for admission.

faq.q: Does the programme cover drone regulations?
faq.a: Yes. The curriculum includes comprehensive coverage of DGCA (Directorate General of Civil Aviation) regulations, international UAV compliance standards, safety protocols, and commercial drone licensing requirements.

From software development and data science to artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, our school prepares international students with the practical skills and industry-ready expertise that technology employers seek worldwide.

about.title: Where Information Becomes Insight
content: The Presidency School of Information Science offers application-centred computing education through three undergraduate programmes and a postgraduate programme, each designed to produce graduates with strong programming, analytical, and software development skills. The school focuses on fields that define the modern technology landscape — data science, artificial intelligence, machine learning, cybersecurity, cloud computing, and full-stack development. With a curriculum structured around the Choice Based Credit System (CBCS) and Outcome Based Education (OBE), students build both breadth and depth across information technology disciplines.

The school has established collaborations with IITs and prominent industry partners for laboratory training, summer programmes, and industry-based certification. A partnership with a Switzerland-based organisation through the Global AI Hub has provided AI and data science certification training to over 2,800 students. Faculty members have been trained at IIT Madras in XR/AR and VR technologies, and the school is developing a Centre of Excellence in Extended Reality (XR). Over 100 research scholars currently pursue doctoral programmes within the school, and a Digital Forensics initiative is being developed in collaboration with the Criminal Investigation Department of Karnataka State. The school’s BCA programme has been ranked 27th nationally among the Top 100 BCA Colleges and Universities.

programs.name: Bachelor of Computer Applications (BCA)

hero.description: Launch your career in software and technology. This three-year programme builds strong foundations in programming, databases, networking, and software development — equipping you with the practical application skills sought by the IT and ITES industries across India and internationally.

overview: The Bachelor of Computer Applications programme is designed to meet the skill requirements of the rapidly growing IT and ITES industries. The curriculum covers computer algorithms and programming, database management systems, computer networks, internet technologies, cloud computing, and mobile application development. Students progress from foundational topics in C programming, applied mathematics, and web development in the first year through Java, database administration, operating systems, and software engineering in the second year, culminating in cloud computing, machine learning, Android development, and a capstone project in the final year.

The programme follows a practice-driven approach, with students introduced to business problem-solving through coding platforms such as CodeChef and HackerRank from the first year. Laboratory sessions, real-world projects, internships, and industry certification pathways ensure that graduates possess both theoretical knowledge and the applied skills that employers value. Bengaluru’s technology ecosystem provides direct access to internship and employment opportunities with software companies, IT services firms, start-ups, and digital agencies.

faq.q: What is CMA-US?
faq.a: CMA-US (Certified Management Accountant) is a professional credential administered by the Institute of Management Accountants (IMA) in the United States. It is recognised globally and valued for roles in corporate financial planning, cost management, and performance analysis.

faq.q: Can I complete the CMA-US certification during the degree?
faq.a: The programme prepares you for the two CMA-US examinations, which you study for during the early semesters. Sitting and passing the examinations during the degree programme is the intended pathway.

faq.q: How does CMA-US compare to CA or ACCA?
faq.a: CMA-US focuses on management accounting, cost management, and financial planning — primarily for corporate finance roles. CA and ACCA focus more broadly on financial accounting, auditing, and taxation for public accounting practice.

faq.q: Is CMA-US recognised in India?

faq.q: Is this programme specifically for students who want to become Chartered Accountants?
faq.a: Yes. This programme is designed for students committed to the CA career pathway. The integrated approach combines academic study with dedicated CA Foundation preparation for maximum efficiency.

faq.q: When can I sit the CA Foundation exam?
faq.a: Students prepare for the CA Foundation examination during their degree programme, with coaching and mock tests integrated into the academic schedule. The specific examination timeline should be confirmed with the school and ICAI requirements.

faq.q: Is the CA qualification recognised internationally?
faq.a: The Indian CA qualification issued by ICAI is one of the most rigorous accounting qualifications globally and is recognised in many countries. It has mutual recognition agreements with several international accounting bodies.

faq.q: What happens if I don’t pass the CA Foundation exam?

hero.description: Combine legal expertise with a deep understanding of society and governance. This five-year BCI-approved integrated honours programme blends political science, sociology, and economics with comprehensive legal education — creating socially sensitive legal professionals equipped for careers in litigation, judiciary, corporate law, and public service.

overview: The integrated five-year B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) programme provides a strong foundation in the political factors that influence policy and legislation, highlighting the intricate relationship between law and social problems. Aimed at creating socially sensitive sentinels of justice, this programme integrates courses in political science, sociology, and economics with comprehensive legal education for students who prefer to approach law from a humanities and social sciences perspective. The honours structure allows students to develop deep specialisation in one of five tracks: Corporate & Commercial Law, Criminal Law, Constitutional & Administrative Law, Technology & IP Law, or International Trade Law.

The curriculum spans ten semesters covering constitutional law, criminal law, contract law, property law, family law, administrative law, international law, labour law, environmental law, and legal research methodology — alongside the humanities foundation. Clinical and practical skills are developed through moot courts, trial advocacy, legal aid clinics, client counselling, ADR (Alternative Dispute Resolution), and mandatory internships. Students develop competence in legal research, communication, and technology — formulating reasoned legal arguments, drafting professional documents, and presenting them effectively using contemporary digital tools and resources.

faq.q: What is the difference between B.A. LL.B. and B.B.A. LL.B. at Presidency?
faq.a: B.A. LL.B. integrates law with humanities — political science, sociology, and economics — suited for students interested in litigation, public service, and social justice advocacy. B.B.A. LL.B. integrates law with business management — economics, accounting, and corporate strategy — suited for students interested in corporate law, business advisory, and commercial legal practice.

faq.q: Can graduates practise law in India?

faq.q: What honours specialisation tracks are available?
faq.a: Five tracks: Corporate & Commercial Law, Criminal Law, Constitutional & Administrative Law, Technology & Intellectual Property Law, and International Trade Law.

hero.description: Deepen your legal expertise and advance your career through postgraduate specialisation. This one-year programme offers advanced study across five specialisation tracks — IPR & Technology Law, International Law, Criminal Law, Corporate Law, and Constitutional & Administrative Law — preparing you for leadership in legal practice, academia, and policy.

overview: The Master of Laws programme deepens legal knowledge across five specialisation tracks, emphasising advanced research, critical analysis, and scholarly writing. The programme prepares students for roles as legal scholars, senior practitioners, policy advisors, and academic leaders. IPR and Technology Law addresses the evolving interface between law and technology — including cloud computing, biotechnological inventions, big data, and traditional knowledge. International Law covers the laws of peace, war, refugees, humanitarian law, and conflict of laws. Criminal Law addresses cyber crime, digital forensics, evidence law, and sentencing reform. Corporate Law covers investment law, international commercial transactions, and commercial arbitration. Constitutional and Administrative Law covers writs, public services, national security, and environmental constitutionalism.

The programme includes practical experience through internships, seminars, and research projects, helping students apply theory to real-world legal situations. Admission is based on merit in the qualifying degree and scores from relevant entrance examinations, combined with an interview conducted by Presidency University's Centre for Post-Graduate Legal Studies (CPGLS). Graduates are well-equipped for leadership roles in legal practice, academia, international organisations, and policy institutions — with expertise in navigating the most complex legal challenges of the contemporary world.

faq.q: What are the eligibility requirements for international LL.M. applicants?
faq.a: Applicants need a 3-year LLB degree or a 5-year integrated law degree from a recognised university with a minimum of 50% marks (45% for reserved categories). Admission is based on qualifying degree merit, entrance test scores, and a university interview.

faq.q: Which specialisation should I choose?
faq.a: Choose based on your career objectives. IPR & Technology Law suits those interested in tech law, patents, and digital regulation. International Law suits those targeting international organisations and diplomacy. Criminal Law suits those interested in prosecution, defence, or criminal justice reform. Corporate Law suits those targeting commercial practice and business transactions. Constitutional Law suits those interested in governance, public interest litigation, and administrative law.

faq.q: Can the LL.M. lead to doctoral research?
faq.a: Yes. The LL.M. provides excellent preparation for Ph.D. programmes in law at Presidency and other institutions, through its emphasis on advanced research skills and scholarly writing.

faq.q: Is the LL.M. recognised for teaching positions?
faq.a: Yes. The LL.M. is a standard qualification for teaching law at universities and law schools in India. It meets the academic requirements for entry-level faculty positions at most institutions.

faq.q: What is the Centre for Post-Graduate Legal Studies (CPGLS)?
faq.a: CPGLS is the postgraduate arm of the School of Law responsible for administering the LL.M. programme, including admissions, curriculum, and research supervision.
